name: AMD's Gigahertz Equivalency: Confused Customers Accept Bad Science
description: Aberdeen Group critiques AMD's Athlon XP Gigahertz Equivalency (GHz-E) marketing strategy, arguing the model-numbering methodology is fundamentally flawed. GHz-E ratings are a snapshot-in-time metric that becomes misleading as benchmarks, operating systems, and Intel processors evolve. The paper documents benchmark disclosure failures (SYSmark 2001 not filed with Bapco), a Media Player bug fix incorporated post-audit, and I/O-inclusive benchmarks misapplied to processor-only comparison. Aberdeen concludes AMD will be forced to abandon GHz-E in 2002 and recommends investigation of SPECcpu 2000 as an alternative framework via the True Performance Initiative.
